ORDER GRANTING MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT

LAW AND MOTION 302, RULING - THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. SPECIALLY APPEARING DEFENDANT ANASON PAK'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT IS GRANTED. THE CROSS-COMPLAINT WAS SERVED ON CROSS-DEFENDANT ANASON WON PAK ("MR. PAK") AFTER THE STAY OF THE ENTIRE ACTION WAS IN EFFECT. (SEE ORDER OVERRULING DEMURRER AND GRANTING MOTION TO STAY FILED JUNE 26, 2013.) THEREFORE, THE SERVICE OF THE SUMMONS AND CROSS-COMPLAINT ON MR. PAK AND THE ENTRY OF DEFAULT ARE VOID. PURSUANT TO CCP 473(D), THE DEFAULT IS SET ASIDE. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY C. VAUGHN, CSR # 6185

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS ALTON MANAGEMENT CORPORATION, PAULA BUTLER, WILLIAM HUTTON'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT - THE COURT OVERRULES THE DEMURRER AND GRANTS THE MOTION TO STAY. STAYING THE ACTION AVOIDS THE RISK OF INCONSISTENT FACTUAL FINDINGS. FOR EXAMPLE, THE ISSUE REGARDING VICARIOUS LIABILITY AMONG THE DEFENDANTS SHOULD BE RESOLVED PRIOR TO AN ADJUDICATION REGARDING INDEMNITY RIGHTS. IN ADDITION, DEFENDANT IS PREJUDICED BY HAVING TO FIGHT LITIGATION ON TWO-FRONTS, AND THIS PREJUDICE OUTWEIGHS ANY SHOWING OF PREJUDICE MADE BY PLAINTIFF INSURANCE COMPANIES WITH REGARD TO THEIR ABILITY TO SETTLE THE UNDERLYING CASE. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, REPORTER: ANTHONY VAUGHN #6185.
ORDER OVERRULING DEMURRER AND GRANTING MOTION TO STAY
CASE MANAGEMENT STATEMENT FILED BY PLAINTIFF FARMERS INSURANCE EXCHANGE TRUCK INSURANCE EXCHANGE

REQUEST FOR JUDICIAL NOTICE IN SUPPORT OF X-DEFTS OPPOSITION TO THE NON-IMPOSITION OF THE STAY FILED BY CROSS DEFENDANT ALTON MANAGEMENT CORPORATION BUTLER, PAULA HUTTON, WILLIAM

LAW AND MOTION 302, CROSS-DEFENDANTS ALTON MANAGEMENT CORPORATION, PAULA BUTLER AND WILLIAM HUTTON'S DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT IS OFF CALENDAR, PER THE JUNE 26, 2013 ORDER GRANTING MOTION TO STAY THIS ACTION. THE REQUESTS FOR JUDICIAL NOTICE ARE GRANTED. THE JUNE 26, 2013 ORDER STAYED THE ENTIRE ACTION, INCLUDING THE CROSS-COMPLAINT. (FURTHER, THE STIPULATION AND ORDER TO STAY CROSS-COMPLAINT AGAINST FARMERS INSURANCE EXCHANGE AND TRUCK INSURANCE EXCHANGE, FILED JULY 22, 2013, IS CONSISTENT WITH THE JUNE 26, 2013 ORDER.) THE RISK OF INCONSISTENT FINDINGS APPLIES EQUALLY TO THE CAUSES OF ACTION BROUGHT AGAINST THE ALTON MANAGEMENT DEFENDANTS, AS THE ALLEGATIONS IN THE CROSS-COMPLAINT ARISE FROM THE SAME CONDUCT COMPLAINED OF IN THE UNDERLYING ACTION (CGC-11-509077). THE ALTON MANAGEMENT DEFENDANTS WOULD ALSO BE PREJUDICED BY HAVING TO FIGHT LITIGATION ON TWO-FRONTS. THE ENTIRE ACTION IS STAYED, PENDING RESOLUTION OF THE UNDERLYING ACTION OR FURTHER ORDER OF THIS COURT. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; COURT REPORTER: SUZANNE CROZIER, CSR # 4761 (302/MJM)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Set Aside Default
Off Calendar Jan-03-2014 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. JEFF M. FACKLER OF MICAEL & FACKLER, TEL. NO. (925) 933-7309 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT;
NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER.

RULING - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. SPECIALLY APPEARING DEFENDANT ANASON PAK's Motion To Set Aside Default is Granted. The Cross-Complaint was served on Cross-Defendant Anason Won Pak ("Mr. Pak") after the stay of the entire action was in effect. (See Order Overruling Demurrer and Granting Motion to Stay filed June 26, 2013.) Therefore, the service of the summons and Cross-Complaint on Mr. Pak and the entry of default are void. Pursuant to CCP 473(d), the default is set aside. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185 =302/MJM

DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-29-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. LAUREN E. POWE OF THE BENNETT, SAMUELSEN & ALLARD LAW FIRM, TEL. NO. (510) 444-7688 APPEARING FOR THE CROSS-DEFENDANTS;
NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER.

RULING - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. CROSS-DEFENDANTS ALTON MANAGEMENT CORPORATION, PAULA BUTLER and WILLIAM HUTTON's DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT is off calendar, per the June 26, 2013 Order Granting Motion to Stay this action. The Requests for Judicial Notice are granted. The June 26, 2013 order stayed the entire action, including the cross-complaint. (Further, the Stipulation and Order to Stay Cross-Complaint Against Farmers Insurance Exchange and Truck Insurance Exchange, filed July 22, 2013, is consistent with the June 26, 2013 order.) The risk of inconsistent findings applies equally to the causes of action brought against the Alton Management Defendants, as the allegations in the Cross-Complaint arise from the same conduct complained of in the underlying action (CGC-11-509077). The Alton Management Defendants would also be prejudiced by having to fight litigation on two-fronts. The entire action is stayed, pending resolution of the underlying action or further order of this Court. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Suzanne Crozier, CSR # 4761 =(302/MJM)

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling RE: DEFENDANTS ALTON MANAGEMENT CORPORATION, PAULA BUTLER, WILLIAM HUTTON'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. the Court overrules the demurrer and grants the motion to stay. Staying the action avoids the risk of inconsistent factual findings. For example, the issue regarding vicarious liability among the defendants should be resolved prior to an adjudication regarding indemnity rights. In addition, defendant is prejudiced by having to fight litigation on two-fronts, and this prejudice outweighs any showing of prejudice made by plaintiff insurance companies with regard to their ability to settle the underlying case. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Anthony Vaughn #6185.
